metadata.description: Kanban board for Channel-Specific Inventory Allocation. Contains original epic task (as reference) and broken-down subtasks (1-2 day units). See ../KANBAN-BREAKDOWN-GUIDE.md for breakdown details.
metadata.note: This file contains the original epic task. Broken-down subtasks should be added based on KANBAN-BREAKDOWN-GUIDE.md

tasks.title: Channel-Specific Inventory Allocation
tasks.description: Implement inventory allocation system allowing stores to reserve inventory for specific sales channels (in-store, online, marketplace).
tasks.userStory: As a store owner, I want to allocate inventory to specific channels so that I can manage inventory across in-store and online sales without overselling.
acceptanceCriteria: Given inventory items, when allocating to channels, then store owner can set quantities reserved for each channel (in-store, online, marketplace)
Given channel allocation, when inventory is allocated, then total allocation cannot exceed available quantity
Given sales occur, when sale happens in a channel, then inventory is decremented from that channel's allocation
Given channel inventory, when viewed, then each channel shows available quantity for that channel
Given allocation changes, when allocations are updated, then changes are reflected immediately across all channels
Given overselling prevention, when channel allocation is exhausted, then that channel shows out of stock even if other channels have inventory
tasks.businessValue: Prevents overselling across channels. Enables strategic inventory distribution.
tasks.technicalNotes: Create ChannelAllocation table to track inventory per channel. Update inventory queries to consider channel allocations. Implement allocation validation.
successMetrics: Overselling prevention: 100% of channel sales respect allocation limits
Allocation usage: 60%+ of multi-channel stores use channel allocation
businessRules: Total channel allocations cannot exceed available inventory
Default allocation: 100% to in-store if not specified
Allocations can be adjusted at any time
When channel allocation is 0, that channel shows out of stock
tasks.note: This is the original epic task. The subtasks should be broken down into 1-2 day units based on KANBAN-BREAKDOWN-GUIDE.md
